seoTitle: Export Pages and Spaces in Confluence (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to export individual pages or entire spaces in Confluence in 2026, choose between PDF, HTML, or CSV, and download a verified backup archive.
intro: Whether you need a single page as a PDF or a full backup of an entire space, Confluence's export tools handle both. This guide walks through exporting individual pages and whole spaces in 2026.

steps.heading: Export a single page
steps.body: Open your target page, click the top-right ellipsis button, and select Export.

steps.heading: Choose a page format
steps.body: Choose PDF or Word, adjust layout sizing, and click Download.

steps.heading: Open space settings for a bulk export
steps.body: Navigate to the lower left sidebar menu and open your Space Settings panel.

steps.heading: Export the whole space
steps.body: Click the Manage Space Data tab, select Export Content, and choose the high-fidelity CSV format.

steps.heading: Apply filters
steps.body: Apply advanced filters to include or exclude specific page clusters, drafts, and system attachments.

steps.heading: Run and download
steps.body: Click the primary Run button, wait for the cloud compilation to finish, and save the complete ZIP file.

faq.question: What's the difference between exporting a page versus a space?
faq.answer: A page export gives you a single document as PDF or Word, while a space export bundles multiple pages, comments, and attachments into a CSV or HTML archive.

faq.question: What format should I choose for a full space backup?
faq.answer: CSV is the high-fidelity option recommended for complete backups since it preserves structural data better than a simple PDF export.

faq.question: Can I exclude drafts from a space export?
faq.answer: Yes, advanced filters let you include or exclude specific page clusters, drafts, and system attachments.

faq.question: How do I verify a large export downloaded correctly?
faq.answer: Open the downloaded ZIP container afterward to verify the text structures and media directories are all present.
